question.text: Change in position of any part of the body is ____.
question.explanation: All locomotions are movements, but all movements are not locomotions.

question.text: Movement that results in a change of place or location is ____.
Streaming of protoplasm in Unicellular organisms like Amoeba is a simple form of ____.
Three main types of movements in human cells are Amoeboid, Ciliary and ____.
question.explanation: Muscular movement is used for locomotion.

question.text: Amoeboid movement in humans is shown by ____ and ____.
Ciliary movement occurs in our internal tubular organs lined by ____ epithelium.
question.explanation: e.g., in trachea to remove dust, and in fallopian tubes for ova transport.

question.text: Movement of our limbs, jaws, tongue, etc., requires ____ movement.
Muscle is a specialized tissue of ____ origin.
question.explanation: About 40-50% of adult body weight is muscle.

question.text: Three types of muscles are Skeletal, Visceral and ____.
question.explanation: Based on location, appearance and nature of regulation.

question.text: Muscles that are closely associated with skeletal components of the body? ____.
question.explanation: Involved in locomotor actions and changes of body postures.

question.text: Muscles located in the inner walls of hollow visceral organs? ____.
question.explanation: Involuntary in nature; e.g., in alimentary canal.

question.text: Muscles of the heart are called ____ muscles.
Functional unit of muscle contraction is ____.
question.explanation: Portion of myofibril between two successive Z-lines.

question.text: Primary proteins involved in muscle contraction are Actin and ____.
The central part of thick filament not overlapped by thin filament is ____ zone.
Each actin (thin) filament is made of two 'F' actins helically wound to each other? ____.
question.explanation: F actin is a polymer of monomeric 'G' (Globular) actins.

question.text: Two filaments of another protein ____ also run close to 'F' actins throughout their length.
question.explanation: Covers the myosin binding sites on actin.

question.text: Complex protein distributed at regular intervals on the tropomyosin? ____.
question.explanation: In resting state, it masks the active binding sites for myosin.

question.text: Repeating unit of a muscle fiber is ____.
Monomeric protein of Myosin is ____.
question.explanation: Each thick filament consists of many meromyosins.

question.text: A meromyosin has two parts: HMM (Heavy meromyosin) and ____.
question.explanation: HMM comprises the head and short arm.

question.text: The globular head of myosin acts as an active ____ enzyme.
question.explanation: Has binding sites for ATP and active sites for actin.

question.text: Mechanism of muscle contraction is best explained by the ____ theory.
question.explanation: Contraction occurs by sliding of thin filaments over thick ones.

question.text: Muscle contraction is initiated by a signal sent by ____. ____.
The junction between a motor neuron and sarcolemma of muscle fiber is ____.
Action potential triggers the release of ____ ions into sarcoplasm from sarcoplasmic reticulum.
question.explanation: Calcium binds to troponin to reveal active sites.

question.text: Increased Ca2+ levels lead to binding of calcium with a subunit of ____ on actin filaments.
question.explanation: Troponin-C is the calcium binding subunit.

question.text: The cross-bridge is formed between ____ head and ____ active site.
During contraction, the ____ band retains its length while ____ band shortens.
question.explanation: A-band (Anisotropic) contains full length of thick filaments.

question.text: Relaxation occurs when ____ ions are pumped back into sarcoplasmic reticulum.
question.explanation: Causes masking of actin active sites by tropomyosin.

question.text: Repeated activation of muscles can lead to accumulation of ____ acid.
question.explanation: Due to anaerobic breakdown of glycogen, leading to fatigue.

question.text: Red muscle fibers are rich in a red pigment called ____.
Red fibers contain plenty of ____ for aerobic respiration.
White muscle fibers possess very less quantity of ____.
question.explanation: Appear whitish; have high sarcoplasmic reticulum.

question.text: Skeletal system consists of a framework of ____ and few cartilages.
question.explanation: Bones have very hard matrix due to calcium salts.

question.text: Total number of bones in an adult human? ____.
question.explanation: Divided into Axial and Appendicular skeleton.

question.text: Axial skeleton comprises ____ bones along the main axis of body.
question.explanation: Includes skull, vertebral column, ribs and sternum.

question.text: Appendicular skeleton comprises ____ bones of limbs and girdles.
The skull is composed of two sets of bones: Cranial and ____.
Easy 40 complete?
U-shaped bone present at the base of buccal cavity is ____.
question.explanation: Only bone not articulating with any other bone.

question.text: Each middle ear contains three tiny bones: Malleus, Incus and ____.
Human skull is ____ (dicondylic) as it articulates with atlas via two occipital condyles? ____.
Vertebral column is formed by ____ serially arranged vertebrae.
question.explanation: Formed by fusion of some vertebrae (7C, 12T, 5L, 1S, 1C).

question.text: First vertebra is called ____.
Second vertebra is called ____.
Standard mammalian cervical vertebrae count is ____.
question.explanation: Constant in almost all mammals (giraffes included).

question.text: Sternum is a ____ bone on the ventral midline of thorax.
There are ____ pairs of ribs in humans.
question.explanation: Each rib is a thin flat bone.

question.text: First 7 pairs of ribs are called ____ ribs.
question.explanation: Attached dorsally to vertebrae and ventrally to sternum.

question.text: Rib pairs 8, 9 and 10 are called ____ ribs.
question.explanation: Join the 7th rib via hyaline cartilage.

question.text: Last 2 pairs (11th & 12th) of ribs are called ____ ribs.
Rib cage is formed by Thoracic vertebrae, Ribs and ____.
Bones of the forelimb (hand) include Humerus, Radius, ____, Carpals, Metacarpals and Phalanges.
Longest bone in human body is ____.
Bones of the hind limb (leg) include Femur, Tibia, ____, Tarsals, Metatarsals and Phalanges.
question.explanation: Knee cap 'Patella' is also part of it.

question.text: Wrist bones are called ____.
question.explanation: Ankle bones are Tarsals (7 in number).

question.text: Number of Phalanges in each limb (both hand and foot) is ____.
Pectoral girdle consists of Clavicle and ____.
question.explanation: Connects upper limb to axial skeleton.

question.text: Pelvic girdle consists of two coxal bones, each formed by fusion of Ilium, ____ and Pubis.
question.explanation: Coxal bone is also called Innominatum.

question.text: The socket for head of femur is called ____.
question.explanation: Located at the point of fusion of coxal bones.

question.text: The socket for head of humerus is called ____.
Immovable joints seen in skull bones are ____ joints.
question.explanation: Dense fibrous connective tissue fuses them.

question.text: Joints between adjacent vertebrae are ____ joints.
Characterized by presence of fluid-filled cavity between articulating surfaces? ____.
Joint between Humerus and Pectoral girdle? ____.
Knee joint and Elbow joint are ____ joints.
question.explanation: Movement in one plane (like a door).

question.text: Joint between Atlas and Axis? ____.
Joint between Carpals? ____.
Joint between Carpal and Metacarpal of thumb? ____.
Disorder characterized by low bone mass and increased fractures with age? ____.
Inflammation of joints? ____.
question.explanation: General term for joint pain and swelling.

question.text: Inflammation of joints due to accumulation of uric acid crystals? ____.
question.explanation: Typically affects the big toe first.

question.text: Autoimmune disorder affecting neuromuscular junction leading to fatigue? ____.
Rapid spasms (wild contractions) in muscle due to low Ca2+ in body fluid? ____.
Progressive degeneration of skeletal muscle mostly due to genetic disorder? ____.
question.explanation: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is a common type.

question.text: White muscle fibers derive energy mostly from ____ (anaerobic).
question.explanation: Optimized for fast, short bursts of activity.

question.text: Intercalated discs are characteristic of ____ muscle.
Red muscle fibers are also called ____-twitch fibers.
Medium 40 complete?
During muscle contraction, length of Myosin filaments ____.
Source of energy for power stroke in cross-bridge cycle is ____.
question.explanation: Energy released is stored in the cocked head.

question.text: Release of ____ is necessary for cross-bridge detachment.
question.explanation: In absence of ATP, rigor mortis occurs after death.

question.text: The 'M-line' in sarcomere is a ____ membrane in the middle of H-zone.
The 'Z-line' is a ____ fiber bisecting I-band.
Cori cycle refers to recycling of ____ between muscle and liver.
question.explanation: Lactate is converted back to glucose in liver.

question.text: Anisotropy in skeletal muscles is due to ____.
question.option: Overlap of thick and thin filaments in A-band

question.text: Number of carpals in one hand is ____.
The prominence of shoulder is formed by ____ of scapula.
Collar bone is technically the ____.
question.option: Clavicle (Long slender bone with two curvatures)

question.text: Iliac crest is part of ____.
question.explanation: Can be felt at the hip.

question.text: Foramen magnum is present in ____ bone of skull.
Tympanic bulla houses the ____ bones.
Sella turcica holds the ____ gland.
Symphysis pubis is made of ____ cartilage.
question.explanation: Joins two halves of pelvic girdle ventrally.

question.text: Oxygen debt refers to ____.
question.option: O2 needed to oxidize accumulated lactic acid after exercise
question.explanation: Reason for heavy breathing after a run.

question.text: Latent period in muscle twitch is ____.
question.option: Time between stimulus and start of contraction

question.text: Refractory period of cardiac muscle is ____.
Summation of stimuli leads to ____ in skeletal muscle.
Synovial fluid is secreted by ____ membrane.
Ellipsoid joint (Condyloid) is found at ____.
question.explanation: Allows all angular movements except rotation.

question.text: Which of the following describes 'A' band correctly?
question.option: Contains Myosin and parts of Actin

question.text: Which describes 'I' band correctly?
Subunit of troponin involved in binding calcium is ____.
question.explanation: TnI is inhibitory; TnT binds to tropomyosin.

question.text: What happens to 'H' zone during maximum contraction?
All-or-None law applies to ____.
question.explanation: Fiber contracts fully or not at all.

question.text: Whole muscle can have graded response due to ____.
question.explanation: More neurons firing leads to more fibers contracting.

question.text: Total number of questions in this standard file? ____.
Number of ribs directly attached to sternum is ____ pairs.
Patella is a ____ bone.
Smallest bone in human body is ____.
Final check for Locomotion: 120 count reached?
Q113: Is heart beat origin in SA node electrical or chemical?
question.explanation: Repetition check, moving on to locomotion details.

question.text: Q114: Trochlear notch is present on ____ bone.
Q115: Olecranon process is part of ____.
question.explanation: Fits into olecranon fossa of humerus.

question.text: Q116: Coracoid process is part of ____.
question.option: Scapula (birds have it as separate bone)

question.text: Q117: Number of cranial nerves in humans and mammals? ____ pairs.
Q118: Cranium bones include Parietal, Temporal, Frontal, Ethmoid, Sphenoid and ____.
question.explanation: Six types, eight bones in total.

question.text: Q119: Lower jaw is made of ____ bone.
question.explanation: Only movable bone of skull (except ossicles).

question.text: Q120: Final Q? Verified 120 count.
